question_text: What is the record for most goals in a single Champions League match?
explanation: Borussia Dortmund 8-4 Legia Warsaw in 2016 produced 12 goals, a Champions League single-match record.

question_text: Which manager has won the most Champions League titles?
explanation: Carlo Ancelotti has won the Champions League 5 times as a manager (2003, 2007, 2014, 2022, 2024).

question_text: Which player has made the most Champions League appearances?
explanation: Cristiano Ronaldo holds the record for most Champions League appearances with over 180 matches.

question_text: Which club has won the most UEFA Champions League titles?
explanation: Real Madrid has won a record 15 Champions League/European Cup titles.

question_text: Which was the first English club to win the European Cup?
explanation: Manchester United became the first English club to win the European Cup in 1968, beating Benfica 4-1 at Wembley.

question_text: Which club is the only one to win the Champions League without losing a single match in the campaign?
explanation: AC Milan went through the entire 1988-89 European Cup campaign unbeaten, winning all their matches.

question_text: In which season was the away goals rule abolished in the Champions League?
explanation: UEFA abolished the away goals rule starting from the 2021-22 season of all European competitions.

question_text: Which club completed a historic treble including the Champions League in 2009?
explanation: Barcelona won La Liga, Copa del Rey, and the Champions League in 2008-09 under Pep Guardiola.

question_text: Who scored the winning goal in the 2005 Champions League final (the "Miracle of Istanbul")?
explanation: Liverpool came back from 3-0 down against AC Milan and won 3-2 on penalties. There was no single "winning goal."

question_text: What was the aggregate score when Liverpool beat AC Milan in the 2005 semi-final?
options: Liverpool didn't play Milan in the semi-final
Liverpool played Chelsea in the semis
explanation: Liverpool beat Chelsea 1-0 on aggregate in the 2005 semi-finals. They faced AC Milan in the final, not the semi-final.

question_text: Which Dutch club won the European Cup three times in a row (1971-73)?
explanation: Ajax won three consecutive European Cups from 1971 to 1973 under coach Rinus Michels and then Stefan Kovács.

question_text: Which player scored twice in the 2018 Champions League Final?
explanation: Gareth Bale came off the bench and scored twice, including a spectacular bicycle kick, as Real Madrid beat Liverpool 3-1.

question_text: Which city hosted the 2023 Champions League Final?
explanation: The 2023 Champions League Final was held at the Atatürk Olympic Stadium in Istanbul.

question_text: In which country is the Champions League final venue typically announced?
explanation: The Champions League final is held at a different venue each year, rotating across UEFA member nations.

question_text: Which country has produced the most Champions League winning clubs?
explanation: England has produced 6 different Champions League winners: Liverpool, Man United, Nottingham Forest, Aston Villa, Chelsea, and Man City — more than any other country.

question_text: What is the record for most goals in a single Champions League season by one player?
explanation: Cristiano Ronaldo scored 17 goals in the 2013-14 Champions League season, a record.

question_text: Which club was banned from the Champions League after winning it in 1993?
explanation: Marseille was banned from defending their 1993 title due to a match-fixing scandal in Ligue 1.

question_text: In which year did the Champions League introduce a league phase format with 36 teams?
explanation: The 2024-25 season introduced the new Swiss-model league phase with 36 teams replacing the group stage.

question_text: Who is the all-time top scorer in Champions League history?
explanation: Cristiano Ronaldo holds the record with 140 Champions League goals.

question_text: Which was the last team to win the European Cup before it became the Champions League?
explanation: Red Star Belgrade won the last European Cup in 1991, before the competition was rebranded in 1992.

question_text: Which player scored a Champions League goal for 6 different clubs?
explanation: Zlatan Ibrahimović scored Champions League goals for Ajax, Juventus, Inter, Barcelona, AC Milan, and PSG.

question_text: Which club did Pep Guardiola manage to win the 2011 Champions League?
explanation: Pep Guardiola managed Barcelona to the Champions League title in 2011, beating Manchester United 3-1.

question_text: Which Scottish club won the European Cup in 1967?
explanation: Celtic became the first British club to win the European Cup in 1967, known as the "Lisbon Lions".

question_text: Which player scored the winning goal in the 1999 Champions League Final for Manchester United?
explanation: Ole Gunnar Solskjær scored the dramatic injury-time winner against Bayern Munich in the 1999 final.

question_text: Which German club won the Champions League in 2013?
explanation: Bayern Munich won the 2013 Champions League, beating Borussia Dortmund 2-1 at Wembley.

question_text: How many teams play in the Champions League group stage (pre-2024 format)?
explanation: The Champions League traditionally featured 32 teams in the group stage before the 2024-25 format change to 36.

question_text: Which club won the Champions League as a wild card entry in 1991-92?
explanation: Barcelona won the 1992 Champions League (first under the new name), beating Sampdoria at Wembley.

question_text: Which Portuguese club won the European Cup in 1961 and 1962?
explanation: Benfica won back-to-back European Cups in 1961 and 1962 under coach Béla Guttmann.

question_text: Which trophy do Champions League winners receive?
explanation: The Champions League trophy is nicknamed "Big Ears" due to its large handles. Its official name is the European Champion Clubs' Cup.

question_text: What was the biggest comeback in Champions League history (aggregate)?
explanation: Barcelona's 6-1 win over PSG in 2017 (6-5 aggregate after losing 4-0 away) is the biggest comeback.

question_text: How many different clubs have won the Champions League/European Cup?
explanation: As of 2024, 23 different clubs have won the European Cup/Champions League.

question_text: Who scored the only goal in the 2023 Champions League Final?
explanation: Rodri scored the only goal as Manchester City beat Inter Milan 1-0 in the 2023 final.

question_text: Which team did Zinedine Zidane score his famous volley against in the 2002 final?
explanation: Zidane scored his iconic volley against Bayer Leverkusen in the 2002 Champions League Final.

question_text: Which English club won the Champions League in 2005 after being 3-0 down at half-time?
explanation: Liverpool came back from 3-0 down against AC Milan in the 2005 final in Istanbul, winning on penalties.

question_text: Which goalkeeper has kept the most clean sheets in Champions League history?
explanation: Iker Casillas holds the record with 57 clean sheets in the Champions League.

question_text: How many Champions League titles does AC Milan have?
explanation: AC Milan has won the European Cup/Champions League 7 times.

question_text: Which team completed the treble (league, cup, Champions League) in 1999?
explanation: Manchester United won the Premier League, FA Cup, and Champions League in 1999 under Sir Alex Ferguson.

question_text: Which club won the first-ever European Cup in 1956?
explanation: Real Madrid won the inaugural European Cup in 1956, beating Stade de Reims 4-3 in the final.

question_text: Which Spanish club won 3 consecutive Champions League titles from 2016 to 2018?
explanation: Real Madrid won three consecutive Champions League titles in 2016, 2017, and 2018.

question_text: What was the Champions League called before 1992?
explanation: The competition was known as the European Cup from 1955 to 1992, when it was rebranded as the Champions League.

question_text: What does UEFA stand for?
explanation: UEFA stands for Union of European Football Associations, founded in 1954.

question_text: What is the Champions League anthem based on?
explanation: The Champions League anthem is based on George Frideric Handel's "Zadok the Priest" coronation anthem.

question_text: Which player has scored the most Champions League goals?
explanation: Cristiano Ronaldo holds the record with 140 Champions League goals.

question_text: Which English club won the Champions League for the first time in 2012?
explanation: Chelsea won their first Champions League in 2012, beating Bayern Munich on penalties in Munich.

question_text: Which club won the treble (league, cup, Champions League) in 2010?
explanation: Inter Milan won the Serie A, Coppa Italia, and Champions League treble in 2009-10 under José Mourinho.

question_text: Which color is predominantly associated with the Champions League branding?
explanation: The Champions League's visual identity is predominantly blue and white with silver starball motifs.

question_text: What shape are the stars on the Champions League logo?
explanation: The Champions League logo features 8-pointed stars arranged in a circle.

question_text: Manchester City won their first Champions League in which year?
explanation: Manchester City won their first Champions League in 2023, beating Inter Milan 1-0 in Istanbul.

question_text: In which year was the Champions League rebranded from the European Cup?
explanation: The competition was rebranded as the UEFA Champions League in the 1992-93 season.

question_text: Which French club won the Champions League in 1993?
explanation: Olympique de Marseille won the Champions League in 1993, the only French club to ever win it.

question_text: Which club has won the most UEFA Champions League titles?
explanation: Real Madrid has won 15 Champions League/European Cup titles, more than any other club.

question_text: What is an "own goal"?
options: A goal from outside the box
A goal scored into your own net
A goal from a free kick
explanation: An own goal occurs when a player accidentally scores into their own team's net, counting for the opposition.

question_text: What is the penalty spot distance from the goal line?
explanation: The penalty spot is 12 yards (approximately 11 meters or 10.97m) from the goal line.

question_text: What was the "Calciopoli" scandal?
options: A doping scandal in Serie A
A match-fixing scandal in Italian football
A referee bribery case in Spain
explanation: Calciopoli was a 2006 Italian football match-fixing scandal that resulted in Juventus being stripped of titles and relegated to Serie B.

question_text: When was the first women's football World Cup held?
explanation: The first FIFA Women's World Cup was held in China in 1991, won by the United States.

question_text: In which year was the first FIFA World Cup held?
explanation: The inaugural FIFA World Cup was held in Uruguay in 1930.

question_text: In which year did Germany win their 4th World Cup title?
explanation: Germany won their 4th World Cup in 2014, defeating Argentina 1-0 in the final in Brazil.

question_text: Which country hosted the 2022 FIFA World Cup?
explanation: Qatar hosted the 2022 FIFA World Cup, the first in the Middle East.

question_text: The "Treble" in English football refers to winning which three trophies?
options: Premier League, FA Cup, League Cup
Premier League, FA Cup, Champions League
Premier League, Champions League, League Cup
FA Cup, League Cup, Community Shield
explanation: The Treble refers to winning the Premier League, FA Cup, and UEFA Champions League in the same season. Manchester United achieved this in 1999.

question_text: The "Hand of God" incident occurred in which World Cup year?
explanation: Maradona's "Hand of God" goal occurred during the 1986 World Cup quarter-final between Argentina and England.

question_text: Which country hosted the first-ever international football match in 1872?
explanation: The first official international match was played between Scotland and England at Hamilton Crescent, Glasgow on November 30, 1872 (0-0 draw).

question_text: What does "VAR" stand for in football?
explanation: VAR stands for Video Assistant Referee, introduced to help match officials with key decisions.

question_text: Which country has appeared in the most World Cup finals without winning?
explanation: The Netherlands have reached 3 World Cup finals (1974, 1978, 2010) without winning.

question_text: What does FIFA stand for?
explanation: FIFA stands for Fédération Internationale de Football Association (International Federation of Association Football).

question_text: The Hillsborough disaster of 1989 led to what major change in English football?
explanation: The Hillsborough disaster, which killed 97 Liverpool fans, led to the Taylor Report recommending all-seater stadiums in the top two divisions.

question_text: In which country was football (soccer) invented?
explanation: Modern football originated in England, with the Football Association established in 1863.

question_text: What is the standard length of a professional football match?
explanation: A standard match is 90 minutes, split into two 45-minute halves.

question_text: What year were red and yellow cards first introduced at the World Cup?
explanation: Red and yellow cards were introduced at the 1970 World Cup in Mexico by referee Ken Aston.

question_text: Which team won the first ever FIFA World Cup in 1930?
explanation: Uruguay won the first World Cup, defeating Argentina 4-2 in the final on home soil.

question_text: What is the term for scoring three goals in one match?
explanation: Scoring three goals in a single match is called a hat-trick, a term borrowed from cricket.

question_text: Which was the first World Cup to be broadcast on television?
explanation: The 1954 World Cup in Switzerland was the first to have matches broadcast on television.

question_text: When were substitutions first allowed in World Cup matches?
explanation: Substitutions were first allowed at the 1970 World Cup in Mexico, with a maximum of two per team.

question_text: When did the Premier League replace the old First Division?
explanation: The Premier League was founded in 1992, replacing the old Football League First Division.

question_text: The "Miracle on Ice" equivalent in football refers to which match?
explanation: The USA's 1-0 victory over England at the 1950 World Cup is considered one of the greatest upsets in football history.

question_text: Who invented the "Cruyff Turn"?
explanation: Johan Cruyff invented his signature move during the 1974 World Cup match against Sweden.

question_text: What shape is a football pitch?
explanation: A football pitch is rectangular, typically 100-110 meters long and 64-75 meters wide.

question_text: What is the offside rule in simple terms?
options: You can't be behind the ball
You can't be ahead of the second-to-last defender when the ball is played to you
You can't stand in the goal area
You must stay in your own half
explanation: A player is offside if they are nearer to the opponent's goal line than both the ball and the second-to-last defender when the ball is played to them.

question_text: Which player holds the record for most goals scored in a single World Cup tournament?
explanation: Just Fontaine scored 13 goals for France at the 1958 World Cup — a record that still stands.

question_text: When was the offside rule first introduced?
explanation: The offside rule has existed since the earliest codification of football rules in 1863, though it has been modified many times.

question_text: The Munich air disaster of 1958 devastated which football club?
explanation: The Munich air disaster killed 23 people, including 8 Manchester United players known as the "Busby Babes".

question_text: When were three points for a win introduced in the English Football League?
explanation: Three points for a win was introduced in England in 1981-82, replacing the previous two-point system to encourage attacking football.

question_text: Which country has won the most FIFA World Cup titles?
explanation: Brazil has won 5 World Cups (1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, 2002).

question_text: What was the "White Horse Final"?
explanation: The 1923 FA Cup Final at Wembley is known as the "White Horse Final" because a police officer on a white horse helped control the massive crowd that spilled onto the pitch.

question_text: Which player was named Best Young Player at the 2022 World Cup?
explanation: Enzo Fernández won the Best Young Player award at the 2022 World Cup in Qatar.

question_text: What was the "Maracanã" originally built for?
explanation: The Maracanã Stadium in Rio de Janeiro was built for the 1950 FIFA World Cup, with an original capacity of nearly 200,000.

question_text: Who scored the famous "Hand of God" goal in the 1986 World Cup?
explanation: Diego Maradona scored the controversial "Hand of God" goal against England in the 1986 quarterfinal.

question_text: How many times has the host nation won the FIFA World Cup?
explanation: The host nation has won the World Cup 6 times: Uruguay (1930), Italy (1934), England (1966), West Germany (1974), Argentina (1978), France (1998).

question_text: Which goalkeeper saved the most penalties in World Cup shootout history (as of 2022)?
explanation: Sergio Goycochea of Argentina saved 5 penalties in World Cup shootouts (1990 edition).

question_text: When were goal-line technology systems first used at a World Cup?
explanation: Goal-line technology was first used at the 2014 World Cup in Brazil after controversial incidents in previous tournaments.

question_text: What is "stoppage time" or "injury time"?
options: A timeout called by the coach
Additional time added at the end of each half
explanation: Stoppage time is additional time added by the referee at the end of each half to compensate for stoppages in play.

question_text: What is a "clean sheet" in football?
options: When a goalkeeper concedes no goals
explanation: A clean sheet means the goalkeeper and defense prevented the opposing team from scoring any goals.

question_text: The "Golden Goal" rule was used in major tournaments between which years?
explanation: The Golden Goal (sudden death in extra time) was used from Euro 1996 to Euro 2004, then abolished.

question_text: What year was FIFA founded?
explanation: FIFA (Fédération Internationale de Football Association) was founded on May 21, 1904 in Paris.

question_text: The "Bosman ruling" of 1995 changed football by allowing what?
options: Foreign player quotas to be removed
explanation: The Bosman ruling allowed players to move freely to another club at the end of their contract without a transfer fee.

question_text: What color card means a player is sent off?
explanation: A red card results in the player being sent off the pitch and their team playing with one fewer player.

question_text: Which English manager introduced the "WM" formation that revolutionized tactics?
explanation: Herbert Chapman developed the WM formation at Arsenal in the 1930s, revolutionizing tactical thinking in football.

question_text: What was "Total Football"?
explanation: Total Football was a tactical philosophy pioneered by Ajax and the Netherlands in the 1970s where outfield players could interchange positions fluidly.

question_text: What was the "Heysel disaster"?
options: A stampede at a Champions League final
A terrorist attack at a World Cup
explanation: The Heysel disaster in 1985 was a crowd crush at the European Cup Final in Brussels that killed 39 people, leading to a ban on English clubs from European competition.

question_text: The "Superga air disaster" of 1949 killed players from which club?
explanation: The Superga tragedy killed 31 people including the entire Torino team, who dominated Italian football at the time.

question_text: Who was the top scorer at the 2018 FIFA World Cup in Russia?
explanation: Harry Kane won the Golden Boot at Russia 2018 with 6 goals, all scored in the group stage and Round of 16.

question_text: How many substitutions can a team make in a standard match (current rules)?
explanation: Since 2020, FIFA has allowed 5 substitutions per team per match across 3 windows (plus half-time).

question_text: What was "catenaccio"?
explanation: Catenaccio (meaning "door-bolt") was an ultra-defensive tactical system popularized in Italian football in the 1960s.

question_text: Which was the first World Cup to be held in Asia?
explanation: The 2002 World Cup was co-hosted by South Korea and Japan, the first World Cup held in Asia.

question_text: Which competition did Pelé call "the war" after violent matches?
explanation: The 1962 World Cup in Chile had particularly violent matches, with Pelé injured in the group stage and calling it brutal.

question_text: The "Football War" of 1969 was between which two countries?
explanation: The Football War was a brief conflict between Honduras and El Salvador, triggered by tensions during 1970 World Cup qualifying matches.

question_text: The "Miracle of Istanbul" refers to which match?
explanation: The Miracle of Istanbul refers to Liverpool's incredible comeback from 3-0 down at half-time to beat AC Milan on penalties in the 2005 Champions League Final.

question_text: When was the back-pass rule introduced, preventing goalkeepers from picking up deliberate passes?
explanation: The back-pass rule was introduced in 1992 to speed up the game and prevent time-wasting.

question_text: Which was the first football club to be listed on a stock exchange?
explanation: Tottenham Hotspur became the first football club to be listed on the London Stock Exchange in 1983.

question_text: When was the first FIFA Ballon d'Or awarded?
explanation: The first Ballon d'Or was awarded by France Football magazine in 1956 to Stanley Matthews.

question_text: Which was the first football match played under floodlights?
explanation: The first floodlit football match was played at Bramall Lane, Sheffield in 1878.

question_text: What is a "derby" in football?
explanation: A derby is a match between two teams from the same city or region, often the most intense fixtures of the season.

question_text: What happens when a game ends in a draw during a knockout tournament?
explanation: In knockout tournaments, a draw leads to 30 minutes of extra time (two 15-minute halves), then a penalty shootout if still level.

question_text: How many players are on a football team during a match?
explanation: Each team has 11 players on the pitch, including one goalkeeper.

question_text: Which country won the first-ever European Championship in 1960?
explanation: The Soviet Union won the first European Championship (then called the European Nations Cup) in 1960.

question_text: The "Battle of Santiago" took place between which two national teams?
explanation: The "Battle of Santiago" was a notoriously violent 1962 World Cup match between Chile and Italy.

question_text: Which European country is known as "Les Bleus"?
explanation: France's national team is nicknamed "Les Bleus" (The Blues) after their blue jerseys.

question_text: England's national team plays home matches at which stadium?
explanation: England plays their home matches at Wembley Stadium in London.

question_text: Which African nation won the 2021 Africa Cup of Nations?
explanation: Senegal won their first AFCON title in 2021, defeating Egypt on penalties in the final.

question_text: Which team is known as "Die Mannschaft"?
explanation: Germany's national team was commonly referred to as "Die Mannschaft" (The Team).

question_text: Which is the only country from Oceania to have played in a World Cup?
explanation: New Zealand qualified for the 1982 and 2010 World Cups. Australia moved from OFC to AFC in 2006.

question_text: Which Asian nation reached the 2002 World Cup semi-finals?
explanation: South Korea reached the semi-finals of the 2002 World Cup as co-hosts.

question_text: Which country has the most appearances in a World Cup without ever winning it?
explanation: Mexico has appeared in 17 World Cups (most of any non-winner) without ever winning the tournament.

question_text: Which country does Son Heung-min represent?
explanation: Son Heung-min represents South Korea and is their most famous current player.

question_text: Which South American nation has a team nicknamed "La Celeste"?
explanation: Uruguay is nicknamed "La Celeste" (The Sky Blue) after their traditional jersey color.

question_text: Which Central American team reached the 2014 World Cup quarter-finals?
explanation: Costa Rica topped a group with Uruguay, Italy, and England, then reached the quarter-finals in 2014.

question_text: Which national team has the longest unbeaten run in football history?
explanation: Italy holds the record with a 37-match unbeaten run from October 2018 to October 2021.

question_text: Which country's golden generation included Laudrup, Schmeichel, and the Laudrup brothers?
explanation: Denmark's golden generation in the late 1980s and 1990s included Peter Schmeichel, Michael and Brian Laudrup.

question_text: How many times has Japan qualified for the World Cup?
explanation: Japan has qualified for 7 consecutive World Cups from 1998 to 2022.

question_text: Which team won the 2021 Copa América?
explanation: Argentina won the 2021 Copa América, beating Brazil 1-0 in the final at the Maracanã.

question_text: What colors does the Mexican national team wear?
explanation: Mexico traditionally wears green jerseys with white shorts for their home kit.

question_text: Which European national team is nicknamed "Les Bleus"?
explanation: France's national team is known as "Les Bleus" (The Blues).

question_text: North Korea achieved which historic result at the 1966 World Cup?
options: Beat Italy in the group stage
explanation: North Korea beat Italy 1-0 in the group stage of the 1966 World Cup, one of the biggest upsets in history.

question_text: Which national team is known as "La Albiceleste"?
explanation: Argentina is known as "La Albiceleste" (The White and Sky Blue) after their jersey colors.

question_text: Which was the first Asian country to reach a World Cup semifinal?
explanation: South Korea reached the semifinals at the 2002 World Cup, co-hosted with Japan.

question_text: Which South American country has won the most Copa América titles?
explanation: Argentina has won 16 Copa América titles (most recently 2024), surpassing Uruguay's 15.

question_text: Which confederation does Australia's national team belong to?
explanation: Australia moved from the Oceania Football Confederation (OFC) to the Asian Football Confederation (AFC) in 2006.

question_text: Who is the all-time top scorer for the Argentina national team?
explanation: Lionel Messi is Argentina's all-time top scorer with over 100 international goals.

question_text: Which team scored the fastest goal in World Cup history?
explanation: Turkey's Hakan Şükür scored after 11 seconds against South Korea in the 2002 World Cup third-place match.

question_text: Which national team does Mohamed Salah play for?
explanation: Mohamed Salah represents Egypt and is their star forward.

question_text: Which team won Euro 2024?
explanation: Spain won Euro 2024, held in Germany, defeating England 2-1 in the final.

question_text: Which was the last host nation to win the World Cup?
explanation: France in 1998 was the last host nation to win the World Cup.

question_text: What is the nickname of the Brazilian national team?
explanation: Brazil's national team is known as "Seleção" (The Selection).

question_text: Which country was the first from outside the British Isles to beat England at Wembley?
explanation: Hungary famously beat England 6-3 at Wembley in 1953, the first non-British team to win there.

question_text: Which team is known as "The Pharaohs"?
explanation: The Egyptian national team is nicknamed "The Pharaohs" after the ancient Egyptian rulers.

question_text: Which country does Erling Haaland play for?
explanation: Erling Haaland represents Norway's national team.

question_text: Who is the all-time top scorer in international football (men)?
explanation: Cristiano Ronaldo holds the record for most international goals with over 130 goals for Portugal.

question_text: Which African nation has won the most Africa Cup of Nations titles?
explanation: Egypt has won the Africa Cup of Nations a record 7 times.

question_text: Which country won Euro 2020 (played in 2021)?
explanation: Italy won Euro 2020, beating England on penalties in the final at Wembley.

question_text: The "Miracle of Bern" refers to which team's World Cup victory?
explanation: West Germany's 3-2 victory over Hungary in the 1954 World Cup Final is known as the "Miracle of Bern".

question_text: Which is the most successful national team in CONMEBOL?
explanation: Brazil is the most successful CONMEBOL nation with 5 World Cup wins and multiple Copa América titles.

question_text: Which national team has the longest unbeaten run in football history?
explanation: Italy went 37 matches unbeaten from October 2018 to October 2021.

question_text: Which country does Kylian Mbappé represent?
explanation: Kylian Mbappé plays for the French national team.

question_text: Which is the highest-ranked CONCACAF team in FIFA history?
explanation: Mexico has historically been the highest-ranked CONCACAF team, though the USA has also reached high rankings.

question_text: Greece won Euro 2004 by beating which host nation in the final?
explanation: Greece shocked the football world by beating host nation Portugal 1-0 in the Euro 2004 final.

question_text: Which CONCACAF team has won the most Gold Cup titles?
explanation: Mexico has won the CONCACAF Gold Cup a record number of times.

question_text: Who is the all-time top scorer for the Portugal national team?
explanation: Cristiano Ronaldo is Portugal's all-time top scorer with over 130 international goals.

question_text: Which African country reached the 2022 World Cup semi-finals?
explanation: Morocco became the first African and Arab nation to reach the World Cup semi-finals in 2022.

question_text: Which nation shocked the world by beating Italy in the 2018 World Cup qualifying?
explanation: Sweden eliminated Italy in the 2018 World Cup qualifying playoffs, causing Italy to miss the World Cup for the first time since 1958.

question_text: Which country has the most Copa América titles?
explanation: Argentina has won the Copa América a record 16 times, including their 2021 and 2024 victories. Uruguay is second with 15.

question_text: Which national team holds the record for the biggest victory in international football?
explanation: Australia beat American Samoa 31-0 in a 2001 World Cup qualifier, the largest win in international football.

question_text: Which country does Luka Modrić captain?
explanation: Luka Modrić is the captain of Croatia's national team and led them to the 2018 World Cup final.

question_text: Which country does Vinícius Jr. represent?
explanation: Vinícius Jr. represents Brazil's national team.

question_text: Which country hosted and won Euro 1984?
explanation: France hosted and won Euro 1984, led by Michel Platini who scored 9 goals in the tournament.

question_text: Which was the first African nation to qualify for a World Cup?
explanation: Egypt was the first African nation to play at a World Cup, participating in 1934 in Italy.

question_text: Which nation won the 1992 European Championship as a last-minute replacement?
explanation: Denmark replaced Yugoslavia (expelled due to war) and went on to win Euro 1992, one of football's greatest upsets.

question_text: Who was the first player to be transferred for over £1 million?
explanation: Trevor Francis became the first £1 million player when he moved from Birmingham City to Nottingham Forest in 1979.

question_text: Which French forward won the World Cup at age 19?
explanation: Kylian Mbappé won the 2018 World Cup with France at just 19 years old, scoring in the final.

question_text: Which player has won the most Ballon d'Or awards?
explanation: Lionel Messi has won the Ballon d'Or a record 8 times.

question_text: Which player holds the record for most career club goals?
explanation: Josef Bican is recognized by some records as having scored over 800 official club goals, though records from his era are disputed.

question_text: Which player captained Spain to their 2010 World Cup victory?
explanation: Iker Casillas captained Spain to the 2010 World Cup title as their goalkeeper.

question_text: Who won the first-ever FIFA Women's Ballon d'Or in 2018?
explanation: Ada Hegerberg (Norway/Lyon) won the inaugural Women's Ballon d'Or in 2018.

question_text: Which player received the first-ever red card in World Cup history?
explanation: Carlos Caszely of Chile received the first red card in World Cup history in 1974.

question_text: Which player is known as "CR7"?
explanation: CR7 stands for Cristiano Ronaldo and his iconic number 7 jersey.

question_text: Which player is the all-time top scorer in La Liga history?
explanation: Lionel Messi is La Liga's all-time top scorer with 474 goals in 520 appearances.

question_text: Who holds the record for most hat-tricks in international football?
explanation: Cristiano Ronaldo holds the record for most international hat-tricks with over 10.

question_text: Which defender has scored the most goals in football history?
explanation: Sergio Ramos has scored over 100 career goals as a center-back, one of the highest-scoring defenders ever.

question_text: Who is the youngest player to score in a World Cup Final?
explanation: Pelé scored in the 1958 World Cup Final at age 17 years and 249 days, making him the youngest final scorer.

question_text: Which club did David Beckham NOT play for?
explanation: David Beckham played for Manchester United, Real Madrid, LA Galaxy, AC Milan, and PSG, but never Bayern Munich.

question_text: Who scored the "Goal of the Century" in the 1986 World Cup?
explanation: Maradona's solo goal against England in the 1986 World Cup quarter-final, dribbling past 5 players, was voted Goal of the Century.

question_text: Luka Modrić won the Ballon d'Or in which year?
explanation: Luka Modrić won the 2018 Ballon d'Or after leading Croatia to the World Cup final.

question_text: Which Italian defender is considered one of the greatest of all time and played for AC Milan?
explanation: Paolo Maldini spent his entire 25-year career at AC Milan, making over 900 appearances.

question_text: Alfredo Di Stéfano played for which national teams?
explanation: Di Stéfano uniquely represented three national teams: Argentina, Colombia, and Spain.

question_text: Who is the all-time top scorer in FIFA World Cup history?
explanation: Miroslav Klose holds the record with 16 World Cup goals across 4 tournaments (2002–2014).

question_text: Which player has scored the most free kicks in football history?
explanation: Juninho Pernambucano scored over 70 direct free kicks in his career, the most in football history.

question_text: Which current player is known for his meditation celebration?
explanation: Erling Haaland's meditation celebration (sitting cross-legged) became famous during his time at Borussia Dortmund.

question_text: Jude Bellingham plays for which club?
explanation: Jude Bellingham joined Real Madrid from Borussia Dortmund in 2023.

question_text: Who was the first player to score in 4 different World Cup tournaments?
explanation: Uwe Seeler and Pelé were among the first to score in 4 World Cups (1958-1970).

question_text: What is Neymar's nationality?
explanation: Neymar Jr. is Brazilian, born in Mogi das Cruzes, São Paulo.

question_text: What position does a goalkeeper play?
explanation: A goalkeeper's primary role is to guard the goal and prevent the opposing team from scoring.

question_text: Which player holds the record for most assists in World Cup history?
explanation: Lionel Messi holds the record for most assists in World Cup history.

question_text: Who holds the record for the most goals in a single calendar year?
explanation: Lionel Messi scored 91 goals in 2012, breaking Gerd Müller's record of 85 set in 1972.

question_text: Who is the only player to win the World Cup, Champions League, and Ballon d'Or in the same year?
explanation: No player has ever won all three — the World Cup, Champions League, and Ballon d'Or — in the same calendar year.

question_text: Which player has made the most appearances in Premier League history?
explanation: Gareth Barry holds the record for most Premier League appearances with 653 games.

question_text: Which player won the Golden Boot at both the 2010 and 2014 World Cups?
explanation: Thomas Müller won the Golden Boot in 2010 (5 goals), and James Rodríguez won it in 2014 (6 goals). No one won both.

question_text: Erling Haaland plays for which English club?
explanation: Erling Haaland joined Manchester City in 2022 and broke the Premier League scoring record in his first season.

question_text: What nationality is Robert Lewandowski?
explanation: Robert Lewandowski is Polish and is Poland's all-time top scorer.

question_text: Which player has won the most Premier League titles?
explanation: Ryan Giggs won 13 Premier League titles with Manchester United between 1993 and 2013.

question_text: Which Brazilian won the World Cup as both a player and a manager?
explanation: Mário Zagallo won the World Cup as a player (1958, 1962) and as manager (1970).

question_text: Which player is the all-time top scorer in Serie A history?
explanation: Silvio Piola holds the record for most goals in Serie A history with 274 goals.

question_text: Who is Brazil's all-time top scorer in World Cup history?
explanation: Ronaldo scored 15 goals across 3 World Cups (1998, 2002, 2006), more than Pelé's 12 goals.

question_text: Which player has scored the most goals in a single edition of the European Championship?
explanation: Michel Platini scored 9 goals at Euro 1984, a record for a single tournament.

question_text: Which country is Cristiano Ronaldo from?
explanation: Cristiano Ronaldo was born in Funchal, Madeira, Portugal.

question_text: Which player famously performed the "SIUUU" celebration?
explanation: Cristiano Ronaldo's signature "SIUUU" celebration (a jumping spin) became iconic worldwide.

question_text: Which Dutch player was known as "The Flying Dutchman"?
explanation: Johan Cruyff was nicknamed "The Flying Dutchman" for his revolutionary playing style and the Cruyff Turn.

question_text: What is Lionel Messi's shirt number for Argentina?
explanation: Lionel Messi wears the iconic number 10 jersey for Argentina.

question_text: Which English player scored a hat-trick in the 1966 World Cup Final?
explanation: Geoff Hurst scored a hat-trick as England beat West Germany 4-2 in the 1966 World Cup Final — the only hat-trick in a World Cup Final.

question_text: Which goalkeeper won the Ballon d'Or in 1963?
explanation: Lev Yashin remains the only goalkeeper to ever win the Ballon d'Or, receiving it in 1963.

question_text: Which German striker broke the Bundesliga single-season goal record?
explanation: Robert Lewandowski scored 41 Bundesliga goals in 2020-21, breaking Gerd Müller's 49-year record of 40.

question_text: Which player is nicknamed "The Egyptian King"?
explanation: Mohamed Salah is nicknamed "The Egyptian King" for his prolific goalscoring at Liverpool.

question_text: Which player won the Golden Ball (best player) at the 2022 World Cup?
explanation: Lionel Messi won the Golden Ball at the 2022 World Cup, capping his career with Argentina's third title.

question_text: Which English striker plays for Bayern Munich and has scored over 200 Premier League goals?
explanation: Harry Kane moved to Bayern Munich in 2023 after becoming Tottenham's all-time top scorer.

question_text: Which club does Vinícius Jr. currently play for?
explanation: Vinícius Jr. plays for Real Madrid, having joined from Flamengo in 2018.

question_text: Which Brazilian is considered one of the greatest players of all time and wore the number 10?
explanation: Pelé is widely considered one of the greatest footballers ever, winning 3 World Cups with Brazil.

question_text: Which player scored 5 goals in a single Champions League match in 2015?
explanation: Luiz Adriano scored 5 goals for Shakhtar Donetsk vs BATE Borisov in the 2014-15 Champions League group stage. Lewandowski's famous 5 goals were in a Bundesliga match.

question_text: Which Brazilian midfielder won two consecutive FIFA World Player of the Year awards in 2004 and 2005?
explanation: Ronaldinho won FIFA World Player of the Year in 2004 and 2005 during his peak at Barcelona.

question_text: Which player scored a bicycle kick goal in the 2018 Champions League Final?
explanation: Gareth Bale scored a spectacular bicycle kick for Real Madrid against Liverpool in the 2018 Champions League Final.

question_text: The "Derby della Madonnina" is between which two clubs?
explanation: The Derby della Madonnina is the Milan derby between AC Milan and Inter Milan.

question_text: Which Portuguese club is known as "Os Dragões" (The Dragons)?
explanation: FC Porto is nicknamed "Os Dragões" (The Dragons) and plays at the Estádio do Dragão.

question_text: Which club holds the record for most goals scored in a single Premier League season?
explanation: Manchester City scored 106 goals in the 2017-18 Premier League season, a record.

question_text: Which club won the first-ever English football league title in 1889?
explanation: Preston North End won the first Football League title in 1888-89, going unbeaten (the original "Invincibles").

question_text: Which Spanish club plays at the Santiago Bernabéu?
explanation: Real Madrid plays their home matches at the Santiago Bernabéu Stadium in Madrid.

question_text: What animal is on the Tottenham Hotspur crest?
explanation: Tottenham Hotspur's crest features a cockerel (rooster) standing on a football.

question_text: What is the name of Tottenham Hotspur's current stadium?
explanation: Tottenham Hotspur Stadium opened in 2019, replacing the historic White Hart Lane.

question_text: What color are Manchester City's home shirts?
explanation: Manchester City wears sky blue home shirts, earning the nickname "The Cityzens" or "Sky Blues".

question_text: Which Italian club plays in black and white stripes?
explanation: Juventus is famous for their black and white striped jerseys, earning the nickname "The Old Lady".

question_text: Nottingham Forest won back-to-back European Cups in which years?
explanation: Nottingham Forest won the European Cup in 1979 and 1980 under Brian Clough.

question_text: Which club plays at Old Trafford?
explanation: Manchester United plays at Old Trafford, known as "The Theatre of Dreams".

question_text: Which team scored the fastest goal in World Cup history (11 seconds)?
explanation: Hakan Şükür of Turkey scored after just 11 seconds against South Korea at the 2002 World Cup — the fastest ever.

question_text: Which English club has never been relegated from the Premier League?
explanation: Arsenal has played in the top flight of English football since 1919 and has never been relegated from the Premier League.

question_text: Borussia Dortmund's stadium is known by what nickname?
explanation: Dortmund's Südtribüne (South Stand) is known as "The Yellow Wall" — the largest standing terrace in Europe with 25,000 fans.

question_text: Which club won the first Premier League title in 1992-93?
explanation: Manchester United won the inaugural Premier League season in 1992-93 under Sir Alex Ferguson.

question_text: Which Japanese club did Andrés Iniesta join after leaving Barcelona?
explanation: Andrés Iniesta joined Vissel Kobe in Japan in 2018 after leaving Barcelona.

question_text: Which club is nicknamed "The Blues" and plays in the Premier League?
explanation: Chelsea is commonly known as "The Blues" after their blue home kit.

question_text: Which club won the first-ever FIFA Club World Cup?
explanation: Corinthians won the first FIFA Club World Cup in 2000, held in Brazil.

question_text: Flamengo is the most popular club in which country?
explanation: Flamengo, based in Rio de Janeiro, is the most supported club in Brazil with over 40 million fans.

question_text: Which club was the first to install undersoil heating at their stadium?
explanation: Everton's Goodison Park was the first English ground to install undersoil heating in 1958.

question_text: Which club's anthem is "You'll Never Walk Alone"?
explanation: Liverpool FC's anthem "You'll Never Walk Alone" is sung by the Kop at Anfield before every match.

question_text: Which club won the Premier League in the 2015-16 season as 5000-1 outsiders?
explanation: Leicester City's 2015-16 Premier League title is considered one of the greatest underdog stories in sports.

question_text: Which club was known as "The Invincibles" for going an entire Premier League season unbeaten?
explanation: Arsenal's 2003-04 squad went the entire 38-match Premier League season unbeaten, earning the "Invincibles" title.

question_text: Which London club is nicknamed "The Gunners"?
explanation: Arsenal is nicknamed "The Gunners" due to the club's origins at the Royal Arsenal munitions factory.

question_text: Which club won the European Cup Winners' Cup the most times before it was abolished?
explanation: Barcelona won the Cup Winners' Cup a record 4 times (1979, 1982, 1989, 1997) before it was abolished in 1999.

question_text: Which club plays at Anfield?
explanation: Liverpool plays at Anfield, their home since 1892.

question_text: Which French club has won the most Ligue 1 titles?
explanation: Paris Saint-Germain has won the most Ligue 1 titles, dominating French football especially since 2012.

question_text: Which London club plays at Stamford Bridge?
explanation: Chelsea plays at Stamford Bridge in Fulham, west London.

question_text: Which Dutch club plays at the Johan Cruyff Arena?
explanation: Ajax plays at the Johan Cruyff Arena in Amsterdam, renamed in 2018 in honor of Johan Cruyff.

question_text: Which club has won the most Serie A titles?
explanation: Juventus has won the most Serie A titles with 36 (though 2 were stripped due to Calciopoli).

question_text: River Plate and Boca Juniors contest which famous derby?
explanation: The Superclásico between River Plate and Boca Juniors in Buenos Aires is one of the fiercest derbies in world football.

question_text: Which MLS team did David Beckham help found?
explanation: David Beckham co-founded Inter Miami CF, which joined MLS in 2020.

question_text: Which club does Lionel Messi currently play for?
explanation: Lionel Messi plays for Inter Miami in MLS, having joined in 2023.

question_text: What is the oldest football club in the world?
explanation: Sheffield FC, founded in 1857, is recognized by FIFA as the oldest football club in the world.

question_text: What is the capacity of Camp Nou?
explanation: Camp Nou has a capacity of 99,354, making it the largest stadium in Europe.

question_text: Which club has won the most FA Cup titles?
explanation: Arsenal has won the FA Cup a record 14 times.

question_text: Which Mexican club has won the most Liga MX titles?
explanation: Club América has won the most Liga MX titles in Mexican football history.

question_text: Which South American club has won the most Copa Libertadores titles?
explanation: Club Atlético Independiente has won the Copa Libertadores a record 7 times.

question_text: El Clásico is a match between which two clubs?
explanation: El Clásico is the famous rivalry match between Real Madrid and FC Barcelona.

question_text: Which club is known as "The Red Devils"?
explanation: Manchester United is nicknamed "The Red Devils", a name adopted in the 1960s.

question_text: Which German club has won the most Bundesliga titles?
explanation: Bayern Munich has won over 30 Bundesliga titles, far more than any other German club.

question_text: What is FC Barcelona's famous motto?
explanation: FC Barcelona's motto is "Més que un club" (More than a club).

question_text: Which Turkish club has won the most Süper Lig titles?
explanation: Galatasaray has won the most Turkish Süper Lig titles.

question_text: What is PSG short for?
explanation: PSG stands for Paris Saint-Germain Football Club, founded in 1970.

question_text: Napoli plays in which Italian city?
explanation: SSC Napoli is based in Naples (Napoli in Italian), southern Italy.

question_text: Which club plays at the Allianz Arena?
explanation: Bayern Munich plays at the Allianz Arena, which opened in 2005 with a capacity of 75,000.

question_text: Which club became the first from the former Soviet Union to win a European trophy?
explanation: Dynamo Kyiv won the Cup Winners' Cup in 1975, the first Soviet club to win a European trophy.

question_text: Which African nation reached the semifinal of the 2022 World Cup — a first for an African team?
explanation: Morocco became the first African nation to reach a World Cup semifinal, defeating Portugal in the quarterfinals.

question_text: What is the maximum number of teams that can qualify for the FIFA World Cup 2026?
explanation: FIFA expanded the World Cup to 48 teams starting from the 2026 tournament, co-hosted by USA, Canada, and Mexico.

question_text: How many total goals were scored in the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar?
explanation: 172 goals were scored across 64 matches at the 2022 World Cup — an average of 2.69 per game.

question_text: Who won the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar?
explanation: Argentina won the 2022 World Cup, defeating France in a penalty shootout after a 3-3 draw.

question_text: Which country won back-to-back World Cups in 1958 and 1962?
explanation: Brazil won consecutive World Cups in 1958 (Sweden) and 1962 (Chile).

question_text: Which continent has never had a World Cup winner?
explanation: No team from Africa, Asia, or North America has ever won the World Cup. Winners have only come from Europe and South America.

question_text: Which goalkeeper has kept the most clean sheets in World Cup history?
explanation: Fabien Barthez holds the record with 10 clean sheets across the 1998 and 2006 World Cups.

question_text: Which player scored the fastest hat-trick in World Cup history?
explanation: László Kiss scored a hat-trick in 7 minutes against El Salvador in the 1982 World Cup.

question_text: How many teams will participate in the 2026 World Cup?
explanation: The 2026 World Cup will be the first to feature 48 teams, expanded from the traditional 32.

question_text: In which year did Spain win their first World Cup?
explanation: Spain won their first and only World Cup in 2010 in South Africa, defeating the Netherlands 1-0 in the final.

question_text: Who scored the fastest goal in World Cup history?
explanation: Hakan Şükür scored just 11 seconds into the 2002 World Cup third-place match against South Korea.

question_text: Who won the Golden Ball at the 2022 World Cup?
explanation: Lionel Messi won the Golden Ball as the best player of the 2022 World Cup in Qatar.

question_text: What was the "Maracanazo"?
options: Uruguay beating Brazil in the 1950 final
explanation: The Maracanazo refers to Uruguay's shock 2-1 victory over Brazil in the decisive 1950 World Cup match at the Maracanã.

question_text: Lionel Messi plays for which national team?
explanation: Lionel Messi represents Argentina and captained them to World Cup glory in 2022.

question_text: Which player was sent off in the 2006 World Cup Final for a headbutt?
explanation: Zinedine Zidane was red-carded for headbutting Marco Materazzi in the 2006 World Cup Final.

question_text: Which country hosted the first FIFA World Cup in 1930?
explanation: Uruguay hosted and won the inaugural FIFA World Cup in 1930.

question_text: Which World Cup match is known as the "Game of the Century"?
explanation: The 1970 World Cup semi-final between Italy and West Germany (4-3) is known as the "Game of the Century".

question_text: Which team holds the record for the largest margin of victory in a World Cup match?
explanation: Australia beat American Samoa 31-0 in a 2001 World Cup qualifier, the largest margin in FIFA history.

question_text: Which was the first Asian country to reach the World Cup semi-finals?
explanation: South Korea became the first Asian team to reach the semi-finals at the 2002 World Cup as co-hosts.

question_text: Which World Cup final had the highest number of goals scored?
explanation: The 1958 World Cup Final between Brazil and Sweden (5-2) produced 7 goals, the most in any World Cup Final.

question_text: What was the official match ball of the 2022 World Cup called?
explanation: The official ball of the 2022 World Cup was called "Al Rihla", meaning "The Journey" in Arabic.

question_text: Which country won the first World Cup to use a penalty shootout in the final?
explanation: Brazil won the 1994 World Cup Final against Italy in the first-ever penalty shootout in a World Cup Final.

question_text: Which country has qualified for every single World Cup tournament?
explanation: Brazil is the only country to have qualified for and played in every World Cup since 1930.

question_text: Who scored the fastest hat-trick in World Cup history?
explanation: László Kiss of Hungary scored a hat-trick in 7 minutes against El Salvador at the 1982 World Cup.

question_text: Which country won the 2014 World Cup held in Brazil?
explanation: Germany won the 2014 World Cup in Brazil, beating Argentina 1-0 in extra time with a goal by Mario Götze.

question_text: What color is the FIFA World Cup trophy?
explanation: The FIFA World Cup Trophy is made of 18-karat gold and stands 36.8 cm tall.

question_text: How many FIFA World Cup tournaments have been held as of 2022?
explanation: 22 FIFA World Cup tournaments have been held from 1930 to 2022.

question_text: Which player has received the most red cards in World Cup history?
explanation: Rigobert Song is the only player to be sent off in two different World Cups (1994 and 1998).

question_text: Which team was eliminated from the 2014 World Cup after losing 7-1 to Germany?
explanation: Brazil suffered a historic 7-1 defeat to Germany in the 2014 World Cup semi-final, known as the "Mineirazo".

question_text: Which South American country has won two World Cups?
explanation: Both Argentina (1978, 1986, 2022) and Uruguay (1930, 1950) have won multiple World Cups, but Uruguay won exactly two.

question_text: Which player scored the "Hand of God" goal?
explanation: Diego Maradona scored the infamous "Hand of God" goal against England in the 1986 World Cup quarter-final.

question_text: Which country has won the most FIFA World Cup titles?
explanation: Brazil has won the FIFA World Cup a record 5 times (1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, 2002).

question_text: What is the name of the World Cup trophy?
explanation: The current trophy is called the FIFA World Cup Trophy, introduced in 1974. The original was the Jules Rimet Trophy.

question_text: How often is the FIFA World Cup held?
explanation: The FIFA World Cup is held every 4 years since its inception in 1930.

question_text: Which African team first reached the World Cup quarter-finals?
explanation: Cameroon became the first African team to reach the World Cup quarter-finals in 1990 in Italy.

question_text: What is the record for most goals scored by one team in a single World Cup match?
explanation: Hungary beat El Salvador 10-1 in the 1982 World Cup, scoring 10 goals in a single match.

question_text: Just Fontaine scored 13 goals in the 1958 World Cup. How many matches did he play?
explanation: Just Fontaine scored his record 13 goals in just 6 matches at the 1958 World Cup in Sweden.

question_text: How many World Cups were cancelled due to World War II?
explanation: Two World Cups were cancelled: 1942 and 1946, due to World War II.

question_text: Which team won the 2018 World Cup in Russia?
explanation: France won the 2018 World Cup, defeating Croatia 4-2 in the final.

question_text: Who is the oldest player to score in a World Cup match?
explanation: Roger Milla scored against Russia in the 1994 World Cup at age 42 years and 39 days.

question_text: Which was the first World Cup to use VAR (Video Assistant Referee)?
explanation: The 2018 World Cup in Russia was the first to implement VAR technology.

question_text: Which country hosted the 2010 World Cup, the first on African soil?
explanation: South Africa hosted the 2010 World Cup, making it the first African nation to host the tournament.

question_text: Which player has appeared in the most World Cup tournaments?
explanation: Antonio Carbajal and Lothar Matthäus both appeared in 5 World Cup tournaments.

question_text: In which year did the World Cup expand from 16 to 24 teams?
explanation: The 1982 World Cup in Spain was the first to feature 24 teams, expanded from 16.

question_text: How many players are on each team during a World Cup match?
explanation: Each team fields 11 players on the pitch during a match, including the goalkeeper.

question_text: Which World Cup saw the "Disgrace of Gijón"?
explanation: The 1982 World Cup match between West Germany and Austria is known as the "Disgrace of Gijón" due to apparent match-fixing.

question_text: What was unique about the 1950 World Cup format?
explanation: The 1950 World Cup used a final round-robin group instead of a knockout final. Uruguay topped the group to win.

question_text: Which city will host the 2026 World Cup Final?
explanation: MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey (New York metro area) will host the 2026 World Cup Final.

question_text: Which player has scored the most goals in World Cup history?
explanation: Miroslav Klose holds the record with 16 World Cup goals scored across four tournaments.

question_text: Which World Cup introduced the golden goal rule?
explanation: The golden goal (sudden death in extra time) was first used at the 1998 World Cup. Laurent Blanc scored the first one.

question_text: Which country has hosted the FIFA World Cup the most times?
explanation: Mexico has hosted twice (1970, 1986) and will co-host in 2026 for a record third time.

question_text: Which country hosted the World Cup despite not qualifying as a team in the previous edition?
explanation: The USA hosted the 1994 World Cup despite not qualifying for the 1990 tournament in Italy.

question_text: How many countries will host the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
explanation: The 2026 World Cup will be hosted by three countries: USA, Mexico, and Canada.

question_text: Which country will co-host the 2026 FIFA World Cup along with the USA and Mexico?
explanation: The 2026 World Cup will be co-hosted by the USA, Canada, and Mexico.

question_text: What was the highest-scoring World Cup final?
explanation: The 1958 final (Brazil 5-2 Sweden) had 7 goals, the most in any World Cup Final. The 2022 final had 6 goals (3-3 AET).

question_text: Who was the top scorer at the 2022 World Cup?
explanation: Kylian Mbappé won the Golden Boot with 8 goals at the 2022 World Cup, including a hat-trick in the final.

question_text: Which European country has won the World Cup four times?
explanation: Germany has won the World Cup four times: 1954, 1974, 1990, and 2014.
